claim: Tech leaders (Dario Amodei, Jensen Huang, Sam Altman) repeatedly make exaggerated claims about AI replacing software engineers; these predictions have failed before
actionable: When evaluating AI impact on your career, discount predictions from people financially incentivized to maximize AI adoption (CEO salaries tied to AI growth)

claim: Only 51% of professional developers use AI tools daily despite 90% of engineering teams having access - adoption gap shows tools available but not integrated into workflows
actionable: Organizational AI adoption requires more than licensing tools; teams need training, standards, and cultural buy-in to use them daily

claim: AI coding assistants require substantial guidance and context engineering to produce reliable results; they cannot replace the software engineering role, only augment it
actionable: Use AI coding assistants as a force multiplier by providing clear architecture, tech stack, requirements context, and validation - don't just vibe code

claim: Intent engineering evolved from prompt engineering to context engineering to structured systems - software engineering principles are being elevated, not eliminated
actionable: Shift from simple prompts to comprehensive context systems: provide architecture, success criteria, validation methods, and decision rationale

claim: Enterprise adoption of AI coding tools is slow due to corporate red tape, procurement, security reviews, and engineer skepticism - not technological limitations
actionable: When deploying AI coding tools in organizations: plan for weeks/months of standardization after training, not days

claim: LLMs hallucinate and introduce security vulnerabilities; engineers must maintain deep code understanding and review AI-generated code thoroughly
actionable: Never deploy AI-generated code without thorough code review; maintain technical understanding of what the AI generates

claim: 21% of AI tool licenses remain underutilized - companies buy tools but don't train teams to use them effectively
actionable: Before investing in new AI tools, ensure your team has dedicated learning time and clear workflows to integrate them
